content: Curiosity-driven intrinsic reward mechanisms (like ICM and RND from Topic A) can be repurposed to actively prioritize which new experiences an autonomous agent stores in its long-term memory, creating a self-organizing memory pruning system that outperforms 2024 SOTA static memory retention rules. This creates a curious agent that automatically grows its memory to prioritize high-uncertainty, high-learning-value data rather than storing redundant or low-information observations.
